content: Comparative analysis of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning reveals that the exploratory performance gap between different curious agents stems largely from unaccounted differences in how each agent encodes and retrieves episodic memory, not just the design of the intrinsic reward signal itself. This creates a new, testable framework for evaluating curious agents that centers architectural memory capacity rather than only reward formulation.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
